title: Tricks and Treats

jokes.text: Elaborate bet rules with increasingly specific restrictions ending with 'cut up bits of sponge'
jokes.setup: Simple bet about drinking anything from the kitchen
jokes.punchline: Hyper-specific exclusion of sponge bits shows overthinking
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing teenage overthinking and paranoia about safety

jokes.text: I DON'T WANT TO MESS UP MY HAIR
jokes.setup: About to do gross drinking challenge
jokes.punchline: Worried about hair, not the disgusting drink
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing teenage vanity priorities

jokes.text: THEN HUM SO YOU CAN'T HEAR US / [HUMMING]
jokes.setup: Need Bill to not hear their plotting
jokes.punchline: Bill obediently hums while they describe ingredients
jokes.explanation: Visual comedy of Bill dutifully humming throughout the gross recipe

jokes.text: Cousin toilet story during recipe preparation
jokes.setup: Adding pickle juice to gross concoction
jokes.punchline: Graphic detail about 10 hours on toilet
jokes.explanation: Gross-out humor typical of teenage boys, predictable but character-appropriate

jokes.text: OH, IT'S BUBBLING!
jokes.setup: Mixed disgusting concoction
jokes.punchline: Chemical reaction making it even worse
jokes.explanation: Visual payoff to the gross recipe buildup

jokes.text: LET ME SEE THE MONEY FIRST
jokes.setup: About to drink horrible concoction
jokes.punchline: Professional attitude about getting paid first
jokes.explanation: Perfect character beat showing Bill's practical nature even in gross situations

jokes.text: IT'S NOT BAD
jokes.setup: Everyone expects him to be disgusted
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment showing Bill's unflappable nature

jokes.text: 'It's not bad.' followed by immediate retching sounds
jokes.setup: Bill bravely claiming the mixture isn't bad
jokes.punchline: His body immediately betraying his words with violent reaction
jokes.explanation: Perfect physical comedy payoff - Bill's stoic denial immediately contradicted by his involuntary physical reaction. Great structure and timing.

jokes.text: Movie title confusion - The Nude Bomb vs dirty movie
jokes.setup: Parents think Sam wants to see dirty movie
jokes.punchline: It's actually a Get Smart movie
jokes.explanation: Classic misunderstanding based on movie title innuendo

jokes.text: Dad's harsh assessment of trick-or-treating as 'begging like a tramp'
jokes.setup: Discussion about Sam being too old to trick-or-treat
jokes.punchline: Reducing Halloween to harsh street reality
jokes.explanation: Brutally honest take on Halloween that's both funny and character-revealing

jokes.text: Devil's Day hair removal foam urban legend
jokes.setup: Scary story about seniors attacking freshmen
jokes.punchline: Daniel reveals it's just a stupid rumor
jokes.explanation: Typical high school urban legend deflation

jokes.text: I was just goofin' on 'em / No, you weren't! / Yes, I was
jokes.setup: Ken caught believing the urban legend
jokes.punchline: Daniel calling out Ken's attempt to save face
jokes.explanation: Awkward teen posturing and face-saving

jokes.text: Eating Lick-a-Maid at 7:30 AM because 'it makes my spit taste like fruit juice'
jokes.setup: Lindsay questions eating candy so early
jokes.punchline: Innocent explanation that's somehow more disturbing
jokes.explanation: Perfectly weird Millie logic that's both innocent and unsettling

jokes.text: Dad threw a rock at Daniel's car
jokes.setup: Car is really loud, dad says you need muffler
jokes.explanation: Great escalation revealing neighborhood tension and Lindsay's embarrassment

jokes.text: But I guess you didn't hear it
jokes.setup: Dad threw rock at loud car
jokes.punchline: Obvious why he wouldn't hear rock over loud engine
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan observation about the obvious

jokes.text: Daniel thinks Millie is eating laundry soap
jokes.setup: Millie eating white powdery candy
jokes.punchline: Looks like soap powder to outsider
jokes.explanation: Visual gag playing on Lick-a-Maid's appearance

jokes.text: Thanks for the candy, Skinny / Dad's right, needs muffler
jokes.setup: Kim being mean to Millie
jokes.punchline: Lindsay awkwardly returning to muffler topic
jokes.explanation: Awkward transition showing Lindsay's discomfort with Kim's meanness

jokes.text: Amelia Earhart of McKinley High comparison
jokes.setup: Lindsay skipping classes
jokes.explanation: Clever counselor metaphor connecting skipping to famous disappearance

jokes.text: Don't turn me into the man / See? It's working already
jokes.setup: Rosso trying to be cool counselor
jokes.punchline: Immediately proves he IS 'the man' by celebrating success
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Rosso's self-defeating cool attempts

jokes.text: Class groans at Crime and Punishment assignment
jokes.setup: Teacher assigns heavy Russian literature
jokes.explanation: Standard student reaction to difficult assignment

jokes.text: Make out with my butt and call it love
jokes.setup: Nick's gross foursome suggestion
jokes.explanation: Sharp comeback with wordplay structure, very quotable

jokes.text: Fake narc story about Brian Stoker
jokes.setup: Serious story about Ken ratting out student
jokes.punchline: Just kidding, got you with same joke
jokes.explanation: Standard misdirection joke playing on social anxieties

jokes.text: Everybody hates their mother / No everybody hates HER mother
jokes.setup: General statement about hating mothers
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay revealing group dynamic and Kim's family issues

jokes.text: If that's what you need to call it in your diary, princess
jokes.setup: Lindsay asks if it's a double date
jokes.explanation: Daniel's dismissive attitude toward Lindsay's innocence

jokes.text: When I hit 13, I became a man / That's only in your temple, not real world
jokes.setup: Neal claiming adult status from bar mitzvah
jokes.punchline: Sam's reality check about religious vs real adulthood
jokes.explanation: Sharp takedown of religious coming-of-age vs actual maturity

jokes.text: Hotdog on a Stick girls working haunted house
jokes.setup: Where Neal wants to go instead of trick-or-treating
jokes.explanation: Perfect teenage boy priorities over candy

jokes.text: Groucho impression: That's the most ridiculous thing I've ever hoird! / Groucho sucks
jokes.setup: Sam does Groucho impression
jokes.punchline: Neal's harsh dismissal of the performance
jokes.explanation: Classic friend dynamic of enthusiasm met with dismissal

jokes.text: Tommy, my secret love from church camp
jokes.setup: Millie has been secretive about someone
jokes.explanation: Perfect Millie character moment with innocent romance

jokes.text: French kissing before 6 months sends you to hell
jokes.setup: Millie explaining her relationship rules
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd religious logic delivered with total sincerity

jokes.text: Didn't want to make you feel bad since you don't have a boyfriend
jokes.setup: Lindsay asks why Millie didn't tell her
jokes.punchline: Innocent but brutal assessment of Lindsay's love life
jokes.explanation: Millie's innocent cruelty delivered without malice

jokes.text: Maybe we should name our band Zarios
jokes.setup: Discussion about Santana naming convention
jokes.punchline: Ken wanting to name band after himself
jokes.explanation: Ken's ego applied to band naming

jokes.text: Monster Mash sing-along with mom
jokes.setup: Mom excited about Halloween preparations
jokes.explanation: Wholesome mother-daughter moment that emphasizes generational gap

jokes.text: [SINGING MONSTER MASH] / HA. / RIGHT? / [GIGGLES]
jokes.setup: Jean enthusiastically singing Monster Mash
jokes.punchline: Harold's reluctant participation and her giggles
jokes.explanation: Jean's Halloween enthusiasm contrasts with Harold's grudging participation

jokes.text: Scott Byron story - trick-or-treating into his 20s
jokes.setup: Warning about being too old to trick-or-treat
jokes.punchline: Cautionary tale ending in failure to launch
jokes.explanation: Perfect Harold doom-and-gloom story with dark ending

jokes.text: Don't bring plastic pumpkin / Not if I eat candy as we go / You puked at bus stop
jokes.setup: Planning trick-or-treat strategy
jokes.explanation: Good character history showing Bill's food-related issues

jokes.text: Hippies inject candy with heroin conspiracy
jokes.setup: Warning about Halloween candy dangers
jokes.explanation: Good 1980 period-specific paranoia and conspiracy thinking

jokes.text: Poo in fun-sized candy bars dipped in chocolate
jokes.setup: Building on candy danger warnings
jokes.explanation: Gross-out humor that relies on shock value over craft

jokes.text: I wrote 'My son's the cutest boy in the whole world' on your costume
jokes.setup: Sam asking if mom ruined his robot costume
jokes.explanation: Perfect mom embarrassment that would mortify any teenager

jokes.text: Looking for Chaplin, only seeing Hitler
jokes.setup: Lindsay trying on fake mustache
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag about mustache ambiguity

jokes.text: This is ridiculous. I look like Tom Selleck!
jokes.setup: Lindsay trying on elaborate mustache costume
jokes.explanation: Visual comedy of teenage girl looking like 1980s TV star

jokes.text: Bionic Woman phone conversation with sound effects
jokes.setup: Bill in costume taking phone call
jokes.punchline: Full commitment to character with bionic sounds
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bill moment showing his total commitment to performance

jokes.text: You look like Richard Benjamin in Westworld
jokes.setup: Harris in robot/cowboy costume
jokes.explanation: Perfect Harold pop culture reference showing his film knowledge

jokes.text: What the hell are you supposed to be? / Mission accomplished
jokes.setup: Harold confused by knife-through-head costume
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan response to obvious costume question

jokes.text: How do you know it isn't real?
jokes.setup: Sam commenting on how real the knife looks
jokes.punchline: Harris suggesting it might actually be real
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor twist on costume observation

jokes.text: If I held you any closer, I'd be in back of ya
jokes.setup: Harold in vampire costume being flirty
jokes.explanation: Dated pickup line that fits Harold's generation and vampire persona

jokes.text: For crying out loud / Don't fall out
jokes.setup: Harold reluctantly putting on costume
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy about ill-fitting women's costume

jokes.text: I'll try not to blow anything of yours from now on
jokes.setup: Daniel telling Kim not to blow the speakers
jokes.explanation: Kim's characteristic crude humor with double entendre

jokes.text: They probably didn't know she was so hot in real life
jokes.setup: Bill wondering why people stare at his costume
jokes.punchline: Harris hitting on Bill in Bionic Woman costume
jokes.explanation: Good awkward humor of Harris flirting with cross-dressed Bill

jokes.text: Her boobs weren't that big at the auto show
jokes.setup: Comparison to real Bionic Woman actress
jokes.explanation: Awkward teenage comment about actress's body

jokes.text: Rich people give cheapest candy - it's how they stay rich
jokes.setup: Debate about which neighborhood has best candy
jokes.punchline: Economic theory about wealth and candy distribution
jokes.explanation: Excellent observational humor with economic logic

jokes.text: I'm allergic to more things than anyone my doctor's ever seen
jokes.setup: Bill listing food allergies
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bill character moment showing his medical uniqueness

jokes.text: Harold vampire scaring kids accidentally
jokes.setup: Harold trying to be friendly in vampire costume
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy of costume backfiring

jokes.text: I own a sporting goods store / Homemade cookies with razor blades
jokes.setup: Harold trying to defuse situation
jokes.explanation: Good period-specific Halloween paranoia escalation

jokes.text: Let's head out to the barn for a roll in the hay
jokes.setup: Harold still in romantic vampire mode
jokes.punchline: Corny sexual innuendo with farm metaphor
jokes.explanation: Corny pickup line fitting Harold's generation and desperation

jokes.text: How are things at ass school? Getting all As
jokes.setup: Ken's sarcastic charm school comment
jokes.explanation: Solid wordplay comeback in character conflict

jokes.text: Aren't you guys a little old? / We're young at heart
jokes.setup: Adult questioning their trick-or-treating
jokes.explanation: Sweet but ineffective defense of trick-or-treating

jokes.text: You look like the fricking Tin Man / I could die from peanuts in circus peanuts
jokes.setup: Criticism of Sam's Gort costume
jokes.punchline: Bill's genuine concern about candy ingredients
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist Bill moment about death by circus peanuts

jokes.text: UM, MA'AM... I HOPE THERE AREN'T ANY PEANUTS IN THESE PEANUTS. 'CAUSE IF THERE ARE, I COULD D-DIE.
jokes.setup: Bill receiving circus peanut candy
jokes.punchline: His logical but absurd concern about peanuts in fake peanuts
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic - worried about peanuts in peanut-shaped non-peanut candy

jokes.text: Cookies covered with ants on lawn
jokes.setup: Jean's homemade cookies being rejected
jokes.explanation: Good visual consequence of Halloween paranoia

jokes.text: Dressed like this? / Stupid kids' holiday
jokes.setup: Jean asking Harold to buy candy
jokes.punchline: Harold's embarrassment about going out in costume
jokes.explanation: Harold's masculine pride vs costume requirement conflict

jokes.text: HAROLD, RUN UP TO FARMER JACK AND BUY SOME CANDY. / WHAT? DRESSED LIKE THIS?
jokes.setup: Jean asks Harold to buy emergency candy
jokes.punchline: Harold's horror at public appearance in vampire costume
jokes.explanation: Harold's vanity about his ridiculous vampire appearance

jokes.text: I'm so happy I'm not at Ted Nugent concert
jokes.setup: Group doing juvenile destructive behavior
jokes.punchline: Ken's sarcastic comment about alternative activities
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ken sarcasm about the evening's 'entertainment'

jokes.text: Oh look, it's fun-sized
jokes.setup: Harris receives Halloween candy
jokes.explanation: Callback to Harris's earlier gross candy warning

jokes.text: That's months of groundwork down the drain / Like they'd ever go out with you
jokes.setup: Neal lamenting Hot Dog on a Stick girls saw them
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Neal's romantic delusions

jokes.text: They always give me free lemonade refills
jokes.setup: Neal defending his chances with the girls
jokes.explanation: Perfect Neal delusion about romantic signals

jokes.text: Just give us your candy / We went through a lot for this
jokes.setup: Bullies demanding candy tribute
jokes.punchline: Sam's earnest defense of their efforts
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Sam's principle vs practicality

jokes.text: I'm not a little girl, I'm a bionic woman / Yeah? Prove it
jokes.setup: Bill defending his costume choice
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of costume vs reality conflict

jokes.text: You're such a little baby / Going to haunted house is better? / Takes one to know one / You are one
jokes.setup: Group arguing about evening activities
jokes.explanation: Good character devolution showing their immaturity

jokes.text: We just egged my little brother
jokes.setup: Group celebrating successful egging
jokes.punchline: Lindsay's horrified realization of victim's identity
jokes.explanation: Perfect dramatic irony moment with real consequences - excellent craft

jokes.text: I told you she'd be a drag
jokes.setup: Lindsay upset about egging her brother
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kim callousness showing her lack of empathy

jokes.text: Like circus freaks? / I don't think bearded ladies run around throwing eggs
jokes.setup: Jean misunderstanding 'freaks' terminology
jokes.explanation: Great generational misunderstanding about 1980s slang vs literal meaning

jokes.text: Last time I had this much fun I was pinned down by North Koreans
jokes.setup: Harold's sarcasm about Halloween disaster
jokes.punchline: Comparison to Korean War combat experience
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor comparing domestic chaos to war trauma - perfect Harold

jokes.text: NOTHING LIKE GETTING YELLED AT BY DAD WHEN HE'S DRESSED LIKE A VAMPIRE, HUH?
jokes.setup: Harold lecturing while still in vampire costume
jokes.explanation: Perfect observation about the absurdity of parental authority in costume

jokes.text: Nobody thinks you're cool, you know / Trust me, I know
jokes.setup: Sam's harsh assessment of Lindsay's new friends
jokes.explanation: Perfect sibling truth-telling moment showing Lindsay's self-awareness

jokes.text: You were supposed to be my little princess / I'll have to be your little prince
jokes.setup: Jean disappointed about wrong costume
jokes.punchline: Lindsay's sweet attempt to make it work
jokes.explanation: Sweet mother-daughter reconciliation moment with gender-bending humor

jokes.text: THAT'S THE WRONG COSTUME. YOU WERE SUPPOSED TO BE MY LITTLE PRINCESS. / WELL, I GUESS I'LL HAVE TO BE YOUR LITTLE PRINCE INSTEAD.
jokes.setup: Jean disappointed about Lindsay's costume choice
jokes.punchline: Lindsay's attempt to salvage the moment
jokes.explanation: Sweet moment of Lindsay trying to make her mother happy

jokes.text: Everyone's name is really weird and long
jokes.setup: Sam reading Crime and Punishment
jokes.explanation: Relatable student complaint about Russian literature

tmdb_overview: Sam convinces Bill and Neal to trick-or-treat on Halloween, but they end up embarrassed while Lindsay and her friends wreak havoc in the neighborhood.
